claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the basics:

**How many children are you enquiring about for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child I'm asking about - my son Alex, who is 7 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm assessing eligibility for your son Alex, who is 7 years old.

Before I can make a determination, I need to understand your living situation better:

**Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to gather a bit more information to complete my assessment.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have a different residency status in the UK?** (For example, are you a citizen of the UK, an EU citizen, or do you have settled or pre-settled status?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I should mention - I don't currently live in the UK. I live abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. That's an important detail. Let me check the specific rules regarding eligibility when living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I can see from the rules that living abroad affects eligibility. According to the information, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you live in the UK. However, there are some exceptions for certain circumstances when living abroad.

I need to ask you a few more questions to determine if any of these exceptions apply to you:

**What country do you currently live in, and what is your nationality or residency status in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen, but I currently live in Spain.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. As a British citizen living in Spain, I need to understand your situation a bit better.

According to the eligibility rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. Spain is one of the countries where you may be able to continue claiming Child Benefit, but there are specific conditions.

**Are you working in Spain, or what is your current employment/activity status there?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not working at the moment - I moved to Spain about six months ago and I'm just living here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I need to understand your circumstances a bit more to assess your eligibility properly.

**Are you receiving any UK benefits at the moment, such as Income Support, Universal Credit, Jobseeker's Allowance, or Pension Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any UK benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. I need to check the specific rules about British citizens living abroad to make sure I have the correct guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I can see from the guidance that the main eligibility requirement states: "You normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK."

Under the "Living abroad" section, it mentions "You may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant." However, the detailed information provided focuses on EU Settlement Scheme status rather than British citizens living abroad.

**Are you a UK citizen by birth, or did you acquire citizenship in a different way?** Also, **do you still have a UK address where you're registered (for tax or other purposes), or have you completely moved your residence to Spain?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK citizen by birth. I've completely moved to Spain - I don't have a UK address anymore. My residence is here in Spain now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Based on the information you've provided, I need to assess your eligibility carefully against the rules.

The key rule states: "You normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK."

The guidance indicates that while you "may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries," the detailed eligibility criteria provided focus on specific circumstances (EU Settlement Scheme status, being a Crown servant, etc.). 

As a British citizen who has relocated to Spain and is no longer residing in the UK, and based on the information available in the eligibility guidance, the fundamental requirement that you "live in the UK" is not met.

However, before I make my final determination, let me ask one more important question:

**Are you a Crown servant (for example, working for the UK government abroad, in the armed forces, or in a similar official capacity)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. As I mentioned, I'm not working at all at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Based on all the information you've provided, I now have enough to make my eligibility assessment.

Let me review your circumstances:
- You are a British citizen by birth
- Your son Alex is 7 years old and lives with you
- You have completely relocated to Spain and no longer reside in the UK
- You are not working and are not a Crown servant
- You are not receiving any UK benefits

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and you live in the UK**. While the guidance mentions that you "may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries," the specific circumstances outlined (EU Settlement Scheme status, Crown servant status) do not apply to your situation as a British citizen living abroad without employment or Crown servant status.

The fundamental eligibility criterion that you live in the UK is not met in your case.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** based on your current circumstances.

**Summary:**

The main reason for this is the residency requirement. Child Benefit is only available to those who live in the UK. While you're a British citizen and your son Alex meets all the other requirements (he's under 16 and lives with you), the fact that you're now permanently residing in Spain means you don't meet the fundamental eligibility criterion.

The guidance does mention that there are some exceptions for people living in certain countries, but these specific circumstances (such as EU Settlement Scheme status or being a Crown servant) don't apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future—for example, if you return to the UK—you would be able to reapply for Child Benefit at that time. If you have any further questions or if your situation changes, you can contact the Child Benefit Office directly for more detailed advice.

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old and lives with you, which normally would satisfy the age and living arrangement requirements for Child Benefit. However, the fundamental eligibility criterion states that you must 'live in the UK' to qualify for Child Benefit. You are a British citizen who has permanently relocated to Spain and no longer reside in the UK. While the guidance mentions that you 'may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries,' the specific circumstances detailed in the eligibility rules (such as EU Settlement Scheme status or Crown servant status) do not apply to your situation. As a British citizen living abroad without employment, Crown servant status, or other specified exceptional circumstances, you do not meet the residency requirement to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You do not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit as you are living abroad (in Spain) rather than in the UK. While you are a British citizen and your son Alex meets the age requirements and lives with you, the core eligibility rule requires that you live in the UK to claim. The exceptions mentioned in the guidance (relating to certain countries and Crown servants) do not apply to your specific circumstances.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 7 years old and lives with you, which normally would satisfy the age and living arrangement requirements for Child Benefit. However, the fundamental eligibility criterion states that you must 'live in the UK' to qualify for Child Benefit. You are a British citizen who has permanently relocated to Spain and no longer reside in the UK. While the guidance mentions that you 'may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries,' the specific circumstances detailed in the eligibility rules (such as EU Settlement Scheme status or Crown servant status) do not apply to your situation. As a British citizen living abroad without employment, Crown servant status, or other specified exceptional circumstances, you do not meet the residency requirement to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: You do not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit as you are living abroad (in Spain) rather than in the UK. While you are a British citizen and your son Alex meets the age requirements and lives with you, the core eligibility rule requires that you live in the UK to claim. The exceptions mentioned in the guidance (relating to certain countries and Crown servants) do not apply to your specific circumstances.
